title: Walmart closes Worcester fulfillment center, laying off 90 employees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Walmart closed its Sam’s Club Fulfillment Center in Worcester, affecting 90 employees as part of a consolidation plan.
Laid-off employees can apply for other company jobs and are offered a $7,500 transfer incentive and possible relocation benefits.
Walmart aims to improve delivery speeds with Next Generation facilities utilizing automation in several states, including an upcoming one in California.

title: Long-planned Nubian Square jazz club finally secures funding
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Jazz Urbane Cafe secures $2.5 million funding to open in Nubian Square, Roxbury.
The cafe, launched by Bill Banfield, will feature local musicians and seats 200 guests.
Funding includes loans from MassDevelopment, M&T Bank, and a guarantee from The Boston Foundation.

title: JetBlue has raised checked bag fees. See how much you’ll pay.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue increased checked bag fees by $4 for off-peak and $9 for peak travel periods.
Minimum fees for the first checked bag are now $39 off-peak and $49 during peak travel.
Travelers paying for checked bags at the airport face an additional $10 fee for both off-peak and peak periods.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to plotting to assassinate Trump Cabinet member
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ryan Michael English, 24, from South Deerfield, pleaded guilty to charges related to plotting an attack on Trump's Treasury nominee.
English was apprehended with a knife and Molotov cocktails near the U.S. Capitol on January 27, 2025.
His plea deal includes potential sentences of up to 15 years and $260,000 in fines for weapon-related charges.

title: Salisbury woman found guilty of murdering boyfriend with antifreeze
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was found guilty of first-degree murder for poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze.
Prosecutors argued Church was driven by jealousy over Fowler's affair; he died two days after ingesting tainted Powerade.
Church's sentencing is scheduled for Thursday; she faces a potential life sentence for the 2022 murder.

title: New England nominees for James Beard Awards 2026: See chefs, restaurants who made the cut
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: No Massachusetts restaurants made the 2026 James Beard Awards nominee list; other New England states are represented.
The Shipwright's Daughter in Mystic, CT, is nominated for Outstanding Chef at the James Beard Awards.
Winners for the 2026 James Beard Awards will be announced on June 15 in Chicago.

title: Dorchester woman wins $2 million on scratch ticket bought at local liquor store
author: John Waller
bullets: Tuyet Le of Dorchester won $2 million on a '50X Cashword' scratch ticket from Savin Hill Wine & Spirits.
Le opted for a cash payout, receiving a one-time pre-tax payment of $1.3 million.
The liquor store at 1051 Dorchester Ave earned $20,000 for selling the winning ticket.

title: She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Caylie McMahon found inspiration in a personal recovery journey now return to the Boston Marathon, defying odds after spinal surgery.
McMahon patiently recovered at Spaulding Rehabilitation, making a comeback from wheelchair to running as part of the Race for Rehab team.
Accompanied by friend Lindsey Foundl, she's raising funds for Spaulding, honoring the hospital's role in her recovery.

title: Patriots opt for line help in latest NFL mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots, with the 31st overall pick, are projected to select Florida defensive tackle Caleb Banks in the first round.
Caleb Banks, despite past injuries, impressed scouts with his performance at the Senior Bowl and NFL Combine.
Patriots' other projected draft picks include Max Iheanachor, Sam Roush, and Ja’Kobi Lane, among others across several rounds.

title: Retired Scituate couple mourned after fatal helicopter crash
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, died in a helicopter crash off Kauai's coast last Thursday.
The helicopter crash also claimed the life of a 40-year-old woman; four passengers and a pilot were on board.
Federal authorities are investigating the crash involving a sightseeing helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation.
